title: crepis vesicaria subsp. erythia
intro: Crepis vesicaria subsp. erythia, also known as Red Hawkseed or Sea Hawksbeard, is a subspecies of the Crepis vesicaria plant, belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). Traditionally, Crepis vesicaria subsp. erythia has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. It is believed to offer several health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving digestion, due to its high content of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The plant can be prepared into various herbal remedies, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat gastrointestinal issues and other conditions.
